reasoning: Decoded instruction-by-instruction, independently. RBARON.MAC:2260-2269 reads `INC MODECT / LDA MODECT / AND I,0F / STA MODECT / LSR / TAX / LDA AX,MCOUNT / BCC 21$ / LDA I,1 / 21$: STA NEWCT`. LSR operates on A (= MODECT&0F): it shifts bit0 into C and leaves A = MODECT>>1. TAX copies that HALVED value into X. On the 6502, LDA affects only N/Z and TAX affects only N/Z — NEITHER touches the carry — so the C set by the LSR survives intact to the BCC. Therefore: X = MODECT>>1, A = MCOUNT[MODECT>>1], and BCC 21$ keeps that value only when C is clear (MODECT EVEN); when MODECT is ODD the fall-through `LDA I,1` overrides it. NEWCT = (MODECT odd) ? 1 : MCOUNT[MODECT>>1]. Exactly as claimed. The dispatch at :2270-2273 (`LDA MODECT / LSR / BCC STPLNE / JMP INITGR`) confirms even=plane, odd=ground, so the table is consulted only on plane slots. MCOUNT is 8 bytes — RBARON.MAC:1298-1299 `.BYTE 4,2,3,2` + `.BYTE 1,3,4,2` — under the header comment :1296 ';TABLE OF COUNTS BETWEEN PLANE/GROUND WAVES'. 8 entries for 16 MODECT values is exactly the MODECT>>1 fan-in the finding describes. Sum = 4+2+3+2+1+3+4+2 = 21 plane waves per 8 ground slots = 72.4% plane / 27.6% ground. Independently corroborated by GMINIT (RBARON.MAC:1217-1222): `LDX I,0 / STX MODECT / LDA AX,MCOUNT / STA NEWCT` seeds MODECT=0, NEWCT=MCOUNT[0]=4 — the game opens on a run of FOUR plane waves, which is only coherent under the >>1 reading. NEWCT sits past the frame gate (see MI-004), so it is decremented once per plane-generation event, i.e. once per wave — the run counts are exact wave counts. Ours: waves.ts:112-115 `MCOUNT[modect % 8]` indexes by MODECT itself, and waves.ts:137-139 advances modect on every decision so isPlaneWave (%2) flips every wave → 1:1. Both errors verified in our source. No radix issue: the whole region is hex (.RADIX 16 at :74; the only decimal island in RBARON.MAC is :6217-6280) and the values 4,2,3,2,1,3,4,2 are radix-invariant anyway.
corrections: Not a numeric error, but worth stating for the implementer: '1 ground' in the cycle means ONE ground SEQUENCE (one INITGR), not one frame — and that sequence is condition-terminated and open-ended (MI-007), not a fixed-length wave. The 21:8 ratio is a ratio of EVENTS, not of time; the ground share of wall-clock is much larger than 27.6% because a ground sequence outlasts a plane wave.

Add the GMINIT seed to the fix: MODECT=0 and NEWCT=MCOUNT[0]=4 at game start (RBARON.MAC:1217-1222). Our INITIAL_WAVE_CLOCK (waves.ts:124) is {modect: 0, countdown: 0}, which fires a wave immediately with no run-length loaded — under the corrected model the opening state must carry NEWCT=4.

reasoning: The structural claim holds exactly. NWPLNE (RBARON.MAC:2241-2245): `LDA PLSTAT+6 / AND I,0C0 / BNE 17$` (a plane is in flight or exploding → return) then `DEC PLSTAT+7 / BNE 17$`. Only once PLSTAT+7 expires does control reach 5$ and, at :2258, `DEC NEWCT`. So PLSTAT+7 is the per-calc-frame countdown and NEWCT is BEHIND it — NEWCT is decremented once per plane-generation EVENT, not once per frame. The ROM's own header comment on the table (:1296) calls MCOUNT a 'TABLE OF COUNTS BETWEEN PLANE/GROUND WAVES' — a wave count. Radix of the reload values, all in the hex region (.RADIX 16 at :74; only decimal island is :6217-6280, and this module DOES use the trailing-period convention elsewhere — `L.OBJ =28.` :462, `L.PFOB =11.` :245 — so its absence here is meaningful): WO.CNT = 5 (:472, hex 5 = 5) and WO.RTN = 10 with NO trailing period = $10 = 16 (:473, used at :2736-2737 `LDA I,WO.RTN / STA PLSTAT+7 ;DISABLE PLANE FOR WO.RTN FRAMES`). Arithmetic against the 96 ms calc frame: 5 x 96 ms = 0.480 s; 16 x 96 ms = 1.536 s. The finding's 0.48 s and 1.54 s are correct. Ours: waves.ts:133-140 decrements `countdown` once per calc frame and reloads it from interWaveDelay (MCOUNT, values 1-4) → 0.096-0.384 s, and main.ts:452 gates the whole block on `enemies.length === 0 && wrecks.length === 0` — verified verbatim, exactly as the finding describes. So MCOUNT (a wave count) is indeed being spent as a frame count, and the ROM's actual frame meter (PLSTAT+7 / WO.CNT / WO.RTN) has no counterpart in waves.ts. Confirmed.
corrections: THE 32-FRAME / 3.07 s GAP IS WRONG — DROP IT. RBARON.MAC:2360-2361 (`LDA I,20 / STA PLSTAT+7 ;GIVE PLANE ^20 FRAMES TO CROSS CENTER`) sits INSIDE STPLNE, one instruction after `STX PLSTAT+6` (:2359) has already put the plane IN FLIGHT. PLSTAT+7 is a DUAL-PURPOSE byte: while a plane exists it is the in-flight center-cross timer, decremented by FORMTN (:3539 `DEC PLSTAT+7`), and it is NOT an inter-wave gap at all — NWPLNE's `DEC PLSTAT+7` is unreachable while PLSTAT+6's D7/D6 are set (:2242-2243). The real inter-wave gaps are only TWO: WO.CNT = 5 frames (0.48 s — drones still exploding, reloaded at :3032 and by INITIAL at :1234-1235) and WO.RTN = $10 = 16 frames (1.54 s — plane escaped/returning, :2736-2737). So the correct range is 0.48-1.54 s, not 0.48-3.07 s, and the '5x to 32x too short' should read '5x to 16x too short'.

The finding's remedy is sound but note the ROM does NOT gate on 'the sky is clear' the way main.ts does: NWPLNE tests only PLANE #0's status byte (PLSTAT+6), not the drones and not the wrecks. Our `enemies.length === 0 && wrecks.length === 0` is a stricter gate than the ROM's.

reasoning: Both halves of the primary claim are literally true and I verified each. ROM: RBARON.MAC:2260-2263 is `INC MODECT / LDA MODECT / AND I,0F / STA MODECT` — the mask is written BACK to MODECT, so MODECT genuinely cycles 0..15. Radix: the line is in the hex region (.RADIX 16 at :74; the only decimal island is :6217-6280), and $0F = 15 either way, so there is no radix exposure. Ours: waves.ts:138 `const nextModect = clock.modect + 1` — unmasked, unbounded. The divergence is real. But it is confirmed as a FACT, not as a defect worth a human's day — see corrections; I very nearly killed this one for overstatement.
corrections: SIZE/IMPACT IS WRONG — this is cosmetic, not a behavior fix. Downgrade `recommendation` from 'fix' to optional hygiene. Every consumer of modect is already modular: isPlaneWave (%2, waves.ts:108), interWaveDelay (%8, waves.ts:114), grmodeForWave (delegates to isPlaneWave, waves.ts:181). There is ZERO observable behavioral difference today and the finding concedes as much ('Today this is latent').

THE STATED CONSEQUENCE IS FALSE. The finding claims that 'once the MODECT>>1 indexing of MI-003 is restored, an unbounded modect walks off the 8-entry table.' It does not. The natural fix — `MCOUNT[(modect >> 1) % 8]` — reproduces the ROM EXACTLY with an unbounded modect: modect 16..31 maps to indices (8..15)%8 = 0..7, which is the identical cycle the masked ROM produces. The walk-off only happens if the implementer drops the modulo, which the existing code already does not do. So the mask does not become 'load-bearing' after MI-003 at all.

THE FLOAT HAZARD IS UNREACHABLE. 'After ~9e15 waves modect stops being an exact integer' — at ~1 wave per second that is ~285 million years of continuous play. Not a hazard; remove it from the justification.

reasoning: PFOBMN decoded line-by-line and it says precisely what the finding says. RBARON.MAC:3269-3293: `PFOBMN: LDX I,N.PFOB / LDA GRNDCT / BNE 10$` (GRNDCT still loaded → skip the exit test entirely), then the OR-accumulate loop at 5$ (:3272-3282) — `LDA I,0 / ORA AX,PFOBJ+8 / ORA AX,PFOBJ+9 / ORA AX,PFOBJ+0A`, with X stepping down by L.PFOB from N.PFOB, so it folds the three ground-object status bytes of ALL FOUR playfield objects into A. Then `AND I,0C0 ;CHECK FOR VISIBLE GROUND OBJECTS / BNE 10$` (:3284-3285 — any object still carrying D7/D6 keeps ground mode alive), `BIT GRMODE / BPL 10$` (:3286-3287), `BIT GREND / BMI 10$` (:3288-3289), and only then `STA GRMODE` (:3290) — and A is provably 0 at that point, because the BNE at :3285 was not taken, so A & $C0 == 0 and A holds the masked (zero) value. It then does `JSR PFSOFF / LDA I,1 / STA PLSTAT+7 ;START PLANES NEXT FRAME` (:3291-3293). Four-way CONDITION, no countdown. The PLSTAT+7=1 write lands exactly in NWPLNE's `DEC PLSTAT+7` gate (:2244), closing the loop with MI-003/MI-004 — the mechanism is internally consistent. Ours: main.ts:462 `if (wasDecision) grmode = grmodeForWave(decisionModect)` — grmode is a pure function of modect and is overwritten by the next decision, so the exit is structurally unavailable, exactly as claimed. The arithmetic checks out: with decisionModect=1 (ground), stepWaveClock advances to modect 2 and reloads countdown = interWaveDelay(2) = MCOUNT[2] = 3; three tick frames plus the decision frame = 4 calc frames x 96 ms = 0.384 s. The finding's '~0.38 s' is right.
corrections: There is a FOURTH exit condition the claim mentions only in passing and the implementer must not drop: GREND (the ground EOL flag, D7) must be CLEAR (`BIT GREND / BMI 10$`, :3288-3289). Ground mode does not self-clear during an end-of-life/crash sequence.

Implementation hook the finding omits: INITGR does NOT touch PLSTAT+7 (:1403-1408 sets only GRNDCT, GTIMER, GRMODE). Ground mode is ENTERED from NWPLNE's dispatch (:2273 `JMP INITGR`) with PLSTAT+7 already at 0, so the next frame's `DEC PLSTAT+7` wraps it to $FF and NWPLNE is effectively blocked (~255 frames) until PFOBMN sets it to 1. The `STA PLSTAT+7` at :3293 is the ONLY thing that restarts planes — that is the wire our port has to build.

reasoning: Verified end to end. INITGR (RBARON.MAC:1403-1408): `LDA I,2 / STA GRNDCT / LDA I,1 / STA GTIMER ;MOUNTAIN OBJECT TIME-OUT COUNTER / LDA I,0C0 / STA GRMODE ;TO GROUND MODE`. Radix-safe: hex region, and 2 and 1 are radix-invariant. The arming path (:3422-3459) decodes as claimed: `BIT GRMODE / BPL 60$` (ground mode only), then at 55$ `LDA GRNDCT / BEQ 60$ / DEC GRNDCT` — so a ground sequence arms exactly GRNDCT = 2 mountains. The group selection is `JSR RANDOM / AND I,3` (→ 0..3) `/ STA TEMP1 / ASL / ADC TEMP1` — ASL of a value <= 3 leaves carry clear, so this is an exact x3 → 0,3,6,9 — `/ STA AX,PFOBJ+7 ;RANDOM PF OBJECT GROUPS`, followed by `LDA I,80 / STA AX,PFOBJ+8 / STA AX,PFOBJ+9 / STA AX,PFOBJ+0A` (:3456-3459) which arms the three ground-target status bytes. Four authored groups x three targets (right/center/left). GTIMER is reloaded to 1 at :3449 for the next mountain. Ours: I grepped src/ myself — there is no GRNDCT, no GTIMER, no ground target of any kind, and landscape.ts's Mountain interface (:45-54) is exactly {scape, depth, x, active} with no target slots. The 'NO_COUNTERPART' class and the 'l' sizing are both honest. Note on the :368-370 citation: those lines ARE inside the dead `.IF EQ,1` block (:267-373), but the finding uses them only as a LAYOUT GLOSSARY (what PFOBJ+8/+9/+A mean), not as a source of a VALUE — which the brief expressly permits. Not a dead-code kill.
corrections: There are TWO arming triggers, not one. The finding says a mountain is armed when 'a mountain falls off the horizon and GTIMER expires'. RBARON.MAC:3426-3429 is `DEC GTIMER / BMI 55$ / CMP I,8 / BCS 60$` — the BMI (GTIMER underflowed) reaches 55$, but so does the fall-through when the mountain's absolute X position is < 8 (i.e. it has scrolled near screen centre; A holds the SPABS'd X from :3424-3425). Either condition arms the mountain. An implementation that models only the timer will under-deploy targets.

PFOBJ+7's low nibble is a GROUP OFFSET (0, 3, 6 or 9 — a base index into a 3-entries-per-group table), not a group NUMBER 0-3. The finding's own text says 'AND I,3 -> 0..3, then x3' correctly, but the phrase 'one of 4 authored target groups' should not be implemented as a 0-3 field: GRDISP reads it back with `AND I,0F` (:3583) and re-derives the offset, and PFOBJ+7's D7 is a separate BLANKED bit.

reasoning: Decoded INDEPENDENTLY of Pair 4's CB-001, and it holds. PLNZD0 (RBARON.MAC:2399-2408): `LDX I,10 / LDA OBJKLD+1 / BNE 2$ / LDA OBJKLD / LSR / CMP I,10 / BCS 2$ / TAX / 2$: LDA AX,PLNLVL / STA GMLEVL`. The LSR is real and it halves OBJKLD before TAX, so the index is OBJKLD>>1. RADIX PROOF for `CMP I,10` = $10 = 16, demanded and delivered: (a) RBARON.MAC declares `.RADIX 16` at :74 and the ONLY decimal island in the module is :6217-:6280 (`.RADIX 10` at :6217, back to `.RADIX 16` at :6281) — line 2404 is far outside it; (b) the module uses the trailing-period convention for decimals and uses it heavily (`L.OBJ =28.` :462, `L.PFOB =11.` :245, `LDA I,10.` :2056) — :2404 has no period; (c) DECISIVE INTERNAL PROOF: PLNLVL (:2478) is exactly 17 bytes, so its last valid index is 16 = $10 — the clamp value and the default `LDX I,10` both land precisely on the last entry. If `10` were decimal 10, indices 11-16 (including the table's ONLY 5) would be unreachable dead data, yet GMLEVL 5 is demonstrably live at :3023-3027, where it selects the aimed-at-player plane. The hex reading is the only self-consistent one. GMLEVL = PLNLVL[min(OBJKLD>>1, 16)]. Thresholds re-derived from the table 0,0,0,0,1,2,2,2,3,3,3,4,4,4,4,4,5: L1 at index 4 → OBJKLD 8; L2 at index 5 → 10; L3 at index 8 → 16; L4 at index 11 → 22; L5 at index 16 → 32. All five of the finding's numbers are correct. And these are from-zero thresholds for real play: the start-of-game path (:5959-5963, `LDA I,0 / LDX I,EAWRTE-OBJKLD / 10$: STA ZX,OBJKLD ;CLR GAME START VARIABLES`) zeroes OBJKLD. Ours: scoring.ts:89-93 `Math.min(Math.floor(objkld), PLNLVL.length - 1)` — indexes by the raw kill count, no shift. Every rung arrives at half the kills. Confirmed; the fix is the one shift.
corrections: OBJKLD IS NOT 'PLANES KILLED' — the port's `kills` counter is the wrong quantity even after the LSR is added. LVLSTP (:2469-2474) is the ONLY incrementer, and it is called from FOUR sites: a lead plane killed (:5584), a drone killed (:5651), a ground/PF object killed (:3904), and — critically — INITBP, every blimp INITIALIZATION (:1416, `JSR LVLSTP ;STEP LEVEL FOR EACH BLIMP`), which is a SPAWN, not a kill. Our main.ts bumps `kills` on a blimp DESTROYED (:427). So the ROM's difficulty ramps on blimps ARRIVING and on ground targets (which we do not have yet, MI-008) as well as on air kills.

Miscited mnemonic: the finding quotes `CMP I,5` at RBARON.MAC:3025; the source says `CPX I,5` (:3024-3027 is `LDA I,0A0 / LDX GMLEVL / CPX I,5 / BCC 10$ / LDA I,0A8`). Substance unaffected. Also, GMLEVL 5 is not the only rung that aims at the player: at :2349-2358 a GMLEVL >= 4 plane already gets a coin-flip (`JSR RANDOM / LSR / BCS 50$`) chance of the aimed 0B8 variant — so difficulty starts biting at level 4, at OBJKLD 22.

The ATTRACT demo does NOT start at OBJKLD 0: RBARON.MAC:2056 is `LDA I,10.` — with a TRAILING PERIOD, so decimal 10 — seeding the attract-mode demo at GMLEVL = PLNLVL[5] = 2. Only a coined-up PLAY game starts at 0. Harmless for the fix, but do not read :2056 as the game-start value.

reasoning: The radix region is PROVEN, in the direction the finding claims. RBARON.MAC declares `.RADIX 16` at :74. The ONLY radix switches in the entire module are `.RADIX 10` at :6217 and `.RADIX 16` back at :6281 — I grepped for `.RADIX` and there are exactly three occurrences, no more. Lines 2480-2481 sit ~3700 lines before the decimal island, squarely in the hex region. Neither operand carries a trailing period, and the module's trailing-period convention is demonstrably LIVE and in use (`L.OBJ =28.` :462, `L.PFOB =11.` :245, `LDA I,10.` :2056, `LDA I,10.` :5654) — so its ABSENCE at :2480-2481 is meaningful, not an accident. `PLANE1: .WORD -100,100` = -$100,+$100 = -256,+256; `PLANE2: .WORD -100,-100` = -256,-256. `.WORD` is a plain directive with no macro multiplier. Corroborated by the surrounding coordinate space, all hex: P.INDP=$1080=4224 (:464), DRINZ=$1600=5632 (:466), MAXDEL=-$180=-384 (:470), P.MNDP=$140=320 (:469). AND — the strongest evidence, which the auditor missed — the ROM ITSELF calls these values an offset, in a comment: FRDRNE (:3511-3528) tests the drone's status for 2 (PARALLEL/formation), then does `CLC / LDA PLSTAT / ADC ZX,PLOBDB ;X LSB (OFFSET FROM PLANE) / STA ZX,PLOBDB ;ABSOLUTE LOCATION` and the same for Y. So PLANE1/PLANE2 are unambiguously OFFSETS FROM THE LEAD, exactly as our DRONE_OFFSETS treats them — only 2.56x larger. Ours: waves.ts:45-48 `Object.freeze([-100, 100])` / `[-100, -100]` as JavaScript DECIMAL literals, and waves.ts:13 sources them from docs/red-baron-1980-source-findings.md with no radix note. The laundered-radix story is correct. 256/100 = 2.56x. Confirmed.
corrections: The copy trace is incomplete in a way that matters for the fix. The drone record is FIRST a full 28-byte copy of the lead: :2364-2366 `LDX I,N.MOB / LDY I,0 / JSR PLNXCG`, and PLNXCG (:3139-3147) copies L.OBJ = 28. bytes from the lead's DB into the drone's. Only THEN are the fields overwritten: +6 = 2 (formation/PARALLEL flight, :2367-2368), +5 = DRINZ/$100 = $16 (Z MSB, :2369-2370 — the drone's DEPTH is reset to $1600, it does not inherit the lead's), and +0..+3 = the PLANE1 offsets (:2371-2375). Our spawnWave (waves.ts:83) spreads the lead and overrides only x/y — it does NOT reset the drone's depth to DRINZ. That is a second, separate divergence sitting in the same six ROM lines.

Semantics nuance for whoever implements this: in the ROM the offsets stay RELATIVE for as long as the drone holds formation (status byte == 2) and are folded into absolute coordinates ONCE, by FRDRNE, at the moment the formation breaks (`LSR ZX,PLOBDB+6 ;FREE DRONE`, :3514). Our code bakes them to absolute at spawn. Changing the constant to 256 is necessary but, on its own, still models a formation that never holds and never breaks.
